TABLE OF CONTENTS
Item 1. Business
Item 1A. Risk Factors
Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis
Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data

Annual Report

Item 1. Business

Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories. The company also sells a broad ecosystem of services that deepen customer relationships and support repeat engagement across devices and software platforms.

Management highlights brand strength, vertical integration, and a large installed base as important drivers of demand. The filing also notes that product innovation and channel execution remain central to the business model.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

The company is subject to intense competition, supply chain disruption, regulatory scrutiny, and changing consumer demand. Revenue can also be affected by macroeconomic conditions, foreign exchange movements, and product transition timing.

Dependence on third-party manufacturing partners and a global component base introduces operational risk. Adverse legal, tax, or trade outcomes could also affect results.

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis

Management says operating cash flow remained strong and supported ongoing investment, capital returns, and liquidity flexibility. The filing describes disciplined spending, continued investment in product development, and a focus on long-term customer retention.

The company continued returning capital to shareholders while balancing strategic investment needs. Management emphasizes that demand, margin performance, and foreign exchange remain important drivers of results.

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data

The audited financial statements show sizable cash balances, meaningful debt obligations, and continued profitability. Notes to the financial statements discuss liquidity resources, debt maturity structure, and share repurchase activity.
